assertions.description: HTMLCanvasElement.prototype.getContext is exposed (the entry point WebGL contexts are created through).
WebGLRenderingContext class is exposed in the global; the spec still defines WebGL even though SwiftShader fallback is gone.
Calling getContext('webgl', {failIfMajorPerformanceCaveat: true}) returns either a hardware-backed WebGL context or null — never a SwiftShader-backed one.
WebGLContextEvent is exposed; canvases dispatch webglcontextcreationerror when WebGL cannot initialise — which is what visitors see when hardware is unavailable now that SwiftShader is gone.
WebGL2RenderingContext is still exposed by the platform; the removal does not affect the spec surface, only the underlying renderer fallback.
